actions.title: Operation Never Say Die: 15 Defendants Charged in $50M+ Los Angeles Hospice Fraud; 221 CA Providers Suspended
actions.description: DOJ and FBI executed arrests and search warrants in the Los Angeles area, charging 15 defendants in connection with more than $50 million in alleged hospice fraud. Eight were arrested, including the owners of a hospice that reported a 97% five-year patient survival rate while billing Medicare for end-of-life care. Healthy beneficiaries were allegedly paid $300 per month in kickbacks to pose as terminal patients. Total provider suspensions in California reached 221.

actions.title: FinCEN Issues Advisory Urging Financial Institutions to Flag Health Care Fraud Schemes
actions.description: The Treasury Department's Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) issued an advisory urging financial institutions to monitor for fraud targeting Medicare, Medicaid, and other federal and state health care benefit programs. The advisory details how transnational criminal organizations are increasingly exploiting these programs through false claims and money laundering, and provides red flag indicators to help banks identify and report suspicious activity. Financial institutions filed 20% more health care-related suspicious activity reports in 2025 compared to 2024. FinCEN also proposed a rule establishing a whistleblower compensation program for tips related to fraud, money laundering, and sanctions violations.

actions.title: Treasury Secretary Bessent Launches Whistleblower Reward Program for Medicare and Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced a new anti-fraud whistleblower program offering up to 30% of recovered fines to individuals who report Medicare and Medicaid fraud, provided enforcement actions result in penalties exceeding $1 million. The program is administered through FinCEN (fincen.gov/whistleblower) and reward payments come from collected fines, not taxpayer funds. The initiative targets an estimated $70 billion in annual fraud losses and begins enforcement efforts in Minneapolis.

actions.title: Telemedicine Company Owner Pleads Guilty to $46M Medicare Fraud Scheme
actions.description: The owner of a telemedicine company pleaded guilty today to organizing and leading a $46.2 million Medicare fraud conspiracy that spanned more than six years. According to court documents, Christopher Harwood, 43, of Fort Lauderdale, Florida, admitted that he owned and operated a telemedicine company called TelevisitMD. Harwood and his co-conspirators targeted Medicare patients through aggressive telemarketing campaigns, inducing them to accept orthotic braces and genetic tests that they did not need. Harwood paid doctors to approve orders for these braces and genetic tests.

actions.title: Texas Fugitive Apprehended and Sentenced to Over 12 Years in Prison for $61M Telemarketing Fraud Scheme Targeting Medicare Beneficiaries
actions.description: A Texas man was sentenced Wednesday to 150 months in prison and two years of supervised release for organizing and leading a $61.5 million health care fraud and wire fraud conspiracy in which thousands of Medicare beneficiaries who were the victims of deceptive telemarketing were sent thousands of orthotic braces, foot baths, and genetic tests they did not need.

actions.title: Federal Jury Finds Mother and Daughter Guilty of Medicare Fraud Scheme and Payment of Illegal Kickbacks
actions.description: SAN DIEGO – A federal jury today convicted a mother and daughter from Melbourne, Florida, of multiple counts of health care fraud, payment of illegal kickbacks, and conspiracy to commit those offenses. The mother, Cindy Justice, was the owner and president of PureScience Rx, a pharmacy located in Poway. The daughter, Ashleigh Davis, was the operations manager at PureScience Rx and was a licensed pharmacy technician.

actions.title: Medicare Home Health Agency Provider Compliance Audit: VNS Health
actions.description: HHS-OIG compliance audit found that VNS Health received at least $2,965,484 in Medicare overpayments for home health services. OIG recommended VNS Health refund the overpayments. VNS Health did not concur with the recommendations but agreed to repay $12,606 in overpayments associated with five claims.

actions.title: Team Rehab Physical Therapy Agrees to Pay Nearly $5 Million To Resolve False Claims Act Allegations Related to Fraudulent Billing Scheme
actions.description: Team Rehabilitation Services, LLC (Team Rehab), which operates approximately 140 physical therapy clinics across Michigan, Illinois, Indiana, Wisconsin, and Georgia, agreed to pay $4,969,494 to settle allegations that it violated the False Claims Act by submitting false claims to Medicare, Medicaid, TRICARE, FEHBP, and the VA for one-on-one physical therapy services that were actually delivered in group settings. The alleged conduct occurred from January 1, 2018 through December 31, 2024.

actions.title: Task Force to Eliminate Fraud and CMS Suspend 70 Los Angeles Hospice and Home Health Providers
actions.description: The Task Force to Eliminate Fraud and CMS suspended 70 hospice and home health businesses in Los Angeles from Medicare and Medicaid reimbursements using AI-driven fraud detection. VP JD Vance and CMS Administrator Dr. Oz announced the action — the first major operational enforcement by the newly established task force. Suspensions were based on irregular patient rosters, duplicate beneficiary enrollments, and overlapping administrative control across multiple provider entities.

actions.title: Pakistani National Residing in Southern California Charged with Fraudulently Billing Medicare Plans
actions.description: The owner of Oregon Clinical Laboratory in Klamath Falls, Oregon, has been charged by criminal complaint with health care fraud after allegedly participating in a scheme to fraudulently bill Medicare Advantage insurance plans for laboratory testing services never performed.

actions.title: Larchmont Woman Sentenced to Nearly 3 Years in Federal Prison for Her Role in Hospice and Diagnostic Testing Fraud that Conned Medicare
actions.description: LOS ANGELES – A woman from the Larchmont area of Los Angeles was sentenced today to 35 months in federal prison for defrauding Medicare out of more than $14 million by submitting fraudulent claims for hospice care and diagnostic testing services that were either unnecessary or not provided at all. Sophia Shaklian, 38, was sentenced by United States District Judge Stanley Blumenfeld Jr., who also ordered her to pay $14,103,043 in restitution. Shaklian pleaded guilty in November 2025 to one count of health care fraud.

actions.title: House Oversight Committee Launches Investigation into California Hospice Fraud, Sends Letter to Gov. Newsom
actions.description: House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer launched an investigation into rampant hospice fraud in California, sending a letter to Gov. Newsom requesting documents on the state's oversight and internal controls. Key findings: LA County saw a 1,500% increase in hospice providers since 2010, with at least $105 million in annual Medicare overbilling. CMS estimates LA County hospice fraud totals $3.5 billion, with 18% of all U.S. hospice billing originating from LA County alone.

actions.title: Florida Doctor Pleads Guilty to Making False Statements in Connection with Multi-Million-Dollar Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Simon Grinshteyn, 52, of Florida, pleaded guilty to one count of making false statements relating to health care matters. Grinshteyn signed orders for medically unnecessary genetic testing and durable medical equipment without having any direct provider-patient relationship, causing Medicare to pay over $3.1 million in fraudulent claims. Sentencing is scheduled for June 24, 2026.

actions.title: Home Health Care Operator Admits Defrauding Missouri Medicaid
actions.description: Camille S. Childress, 41, of St. Louis County, pleaded guilty to one count of health care fraud for defrauding Missouri Medicaid through her home health care company, Inspiring Angels LLC. Childress concealed her ownership of the company due to a 2012 criminal conviction and submitted fraudulent claims for home health care services that were never provided. Missouri Medicaid paid at least $174,496 on false claims submitted in 2021 and 2022, though Childress was originally accused of defrauding the program out of more than $3 million over more than a decade. She faces up to 10 years in prison and fines up to $250,000, with sentencing scheduled for June 16, 2026.

actions.title: Center for Vein Restoration Agrees to Pay $4 Million to Resolve False Claims Act Allegations
actions.description: CVR Management, LLC, doing business as Center for Vein Restoration, Center for Vascular Medicine, and Dr. Sanjiv Lakhanpal agreed to pay $4 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that they billed Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE for medically unnecessary vein treatment procedures.

actions.title: Azerbaijani National Charged in $90 Million Medicare Advantage Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Anar Rustamov, a national of Azerbaijan who appears to have entered the U.S. illegally, was indicted and charged with 14 counts of health care fraud and money laundering for orchestrating a scheme involving thousands of false claims for medical equipment (blood glucose monitors, orthotic braces) submitted to Medicare Advantage programs, totaling over $90 million. Rustamov operated through an entity called Dublin Helping Hand.

actions.title: Alabama Doctor Sentenced to Over One Year in Prison for $2.7 Million Telemedicine Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Tommie Robinson, a 44-year-old Alabama doctor, was sentenced to 16 months in prison for operating a telemedicine fraud scheme that defrauded health care programs of $2.7 million. Robinson submitted claims for medically unnecessary durable medical equipment and genetic testing through telemedicine services. He pleaded guilty to one count of health care fraud and was ordered to pay $2,784,733.49 in restitution.

actions.title: Mississippi Man Ordered to Pay $31 Million for Role in Health Care Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Robert L. Crites, 67, of Batesville, Mississippi, was ordered to pay $31,039,134.82 in a federal civil judgment for orchestrating an illegal kickback scheme. According to court records, Crites and others associated with Extraordinary Scripts participated in an illegal kickback scheme in which they identified and referred patients across the country to Cloverland Pharmacy in Montgomery, Alabama. Crites primarily recruited beneficiaries of TRICARE, the health care program for members of the U.S. military and their families. In turn, the pharmacy paid Crites and his co-conspirators kickbacks for each referral.

actions.title: Owner of Now-Closed Milwaukee Prenatal Care Coordination Company Sentenced to 60 Months’ Imprisonment for Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Lakia Jackson, 36, was sentenced to 60 months’ incarceration for committing a health care fraud scheme through which she stole $2,655,463.63 from a Medicaid benefit meant to help at-risk pregnant women and women with young children. When Jackson pleaded guilty, she acknowledged that she engaged in a scheme to defraud Wisconsin Medicaid by, among other things, offering women kickbacks in exchange for their Medicaid numbers and falsely billing Wisconsin Medicaid as though she provided reimbursable services—almost always the maximum permitted per month under the program—when, in fact, she and her employees provided few to no covered services.

actions.title: Owner of Now-Closed Milwaukee Prenatal Care Coordination Company Sentenced to 121 Months’ Imprisonment for Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Markita Barnes, 33, was sentenced to 121 months’ incarceration for committing a health care fraud scheme through which she stole $2,361,799.17 from a Medicaid benefit program meant to help at-risk pregnant women and women with young children. In announcing the sentence, Chief United States District Judge Pamela Pepper discussed the substantial negative impact Barnes’ fraud scheme, and others like it in the Milwaukee area, has had not only on public trust in government benefit programs, but on the availability of the benefit program she defrauded. Chief Judge Pepper noted that, because of significant fraud surrounding this benefit program, the state of Wisconsin has scaled back the benefit to such an extent that it is largely not available to the women who actually need it.

actions.title: Consent Judgment Entered Against Bucks County Company Resolving Allegations of False Claims for Billing Group Art Classes in Assisted Living and Adult Day Facilities as Occupational Therapy
actions.description: A consent judgment was entered against Segal Arts LLC and owner Irina Segal of Bucks County, Pennsylvania, for submitting false Medicare claims for one-on-one occupational therapy services that were actually group art-and-crafts sessions at assisted living and adult day facilities in Pennsylvania and New Jersey. The company billed Medicare for medically necessary therapeutic exercises that were never delivered.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: Medicaid Fraud Control Units Annual Report FY2025 — $2 Billion in Combined Recoveries
actions.description: HHS-OIG released its annual report on Medicaid Fraud Control Units (MFCUs), reporting combined criminal and civil recoveries of nearly $2 billion for fiscal year 2025. Criminal recoveries from convictions totaled $1.3 billion and civil recoveries totaled $706 million across all state MFCUs.

actions.title: House E&C Hearing: Protecting Patients and Safeguarding Taxpayer Dollars — The Role of CMS in Combatting Medicare and Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: House Energy & Commerce Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations held a hearing on the Trump Administration's efforts to proactively tackle Medicare and Medicaid fraud, with testimony on CMS enforcement actions, the CRUSH initiative, and vulnerable programs targeted by fraud schemes.

actions.title: Dr. Oz Sends Fraud Inquiry to Florida Governor DeSantis, Calling State a Hotspot for Health Care Fraud
actions.description: CMS Administrator Dr. Oz sent a detailed fraud inquiry to Florida Governor Ron DeSantis, stating Florida has been a hotspot for health care fraud for years. Oz cited DME fraud, a 6 million telemedicine billing scheme, non-emergency medical transportation fraud, and ABA services fraud. Florida was given 30 days to respond. This was the first such letter sent to a Republican-led state; prior letters went to New York, Minnesota, Maine, and California.

actions.title: President Trump Establishes Task Force to Eliminate Fraud via Executive Order
actions.description: President Trump signed an Executive Order creating the Task Force to Eliminate Fraud, chaired by Vice President J.D. Vance, to coordinate government-wide efforts to combat fraud, waste, and abuse in federal benefit programs including housing, food, medical care, and cash assistance. The FTC Chairman serves as Vice Chair. The Task Force will coordinate eligibility verification, pre-payment controls, fraud detection, and minimum anti-fraud requirements. The EO cites Medicaid vulnerabilities in Minnesota, California, Illinois, New York, Maine, and Colorado.
actions.state: MN, CA, IL, NY, ME, CO

actions.title: Safe Chain Solutions Owners Sentenced to 38 Combined Years for $92M Black-Market HIV Drug Scheme
actions.description: Patrick Boyd, 47, and Charles Boyd, 43, of Easton, Maryland, were sentenced to 18 and 20 years in prison respectively for distributing more than $92.8 million of black-market HIV drugs through their pharmaceutical wholesale company Safe Chain Solutions. The brothers bought HIV medications from black-market suppliers through unlawful buy-back arrangements, removed or altered original prescription labels, and falsified documentation to make products appear legitimate before reselling them into the legitimate pharmaceutical supply chain. Co-owner Adam Brosius previously pleaded guilty and was sentenced to 97 months. Forfeiture of $21.85 million was ordered. Prosecuted in the Southern District of Florida.

actions.title: Arizona Cardiology Group to Pay $4.75M to Resolve Allegations of Unnecessary Vein Ablations
actions.description: An Arizona cardiology group agreed to pay $4.75 million to settle allegations that it performed medically unnecessary vascular vein ablation procedures and billed federal health care programs.

actions.title: Aetna Agrees to Pay $117.7 Million to Resolve False Claims Act Allegations
actions.description: Aetna Inc. agreed to pay $117.7 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that it submitted inaccurate diagnosis codes to Medicare to inflate risk adjustment payments. Aetna operated a chart review program that identified unsupported diagnoses but failed to withdraw them, and submitted false morbid obesity diagnoses (2018-2023) for beneficiaries whose BMI readings did not support such coding. The settlement resolves allegations spanning payment years 2015 and 2018-2023. The case was brought by whistleblower Mary Melette Thomas, a former Aetna risk-adjustment coding auditor.

actions.title: Former Greenville CEO and Employees Indicted in Multi-Million Dollar Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: A former CEO and employees of a Greenville health care facility were indicted for their roles in a multi-million dollar health care fraud scheme involving COVID-19 pandemic benefits. The indictment alleges the defendants devised a multi-part scheme to fraudulently generate revenue from health care benefit programs related to the pandemic. This included submitting false claims to the federal government for individual tests when in truth the tests had been pooled together for combined, faster processing, and manipulating test processing software. As a result, the defendants billed for tests that were virtually worthless and ineligible for reimbursement. The conspiracy to defraud health care benefit programs generated millions in fraudulent proceeds.

actions.title: Pennsylvania Brothers Convicted of Decades Long Racketeering Conspiracy
actions.description: Three individuals were convicted at trial in connection with a racketeering conspiracy including health care fraud billing schemes resulting in over $32 million in losses to Pennsylvania Medicaid.

actions.title: Chiropractor Sentenced to 43 Months in Prison for $14.9 Million Health Care Fraud and Kickback Scheme
actions.description: A chiropractor was sentenced to 43 months in federal prison for orchestrating a $14.9 million health care fraud and illegal kickback scheme involving durable medical equipment and genetic testing.

actions.title: Owner of Durable Medical Equipment Company Sentenced for $59 Million Medicare Fraud
actions.description: The owner of a durable medical equipment company was sentenced to federal prison for perpetrating a $59 million Medicare fraud scheme involving fraudulent DME billing.

actions.title: Great Neck, NY Doctor Sentenced to 7 Years for Operating Oxycodone Pill Mill
actions.description: Dr. Roya Jafari-Hassad of Great Neck, NY sentenced to 7 years for operating an oxycodone pill mill from her family practice, making hundreds of thousands of dollars selling prescriptions under the table from 2019 to 2022. Ordered to pay $150,000 fine and $152,765 in restitution. She also submitted false and fraudulent claims to Medicare and private benefit programs for medical services that were not rendered, and charged insurance companies – and was, in turn, paid for – services that she did not provide.

actions.title: House E&C Expands Medicaid Fraud Investigation to 10 Additional States
actions.description: House Energy & Commerce Committee leaders sent letters to 10 additional states (CA, CO, MA, ME, NE, NY, OR, PA, VT, WA) requesting information on Medicaid fraud and program integrity measures. Expansion follows the Feb 3 hearing on Medicare and Medicaid fraud schemes and ongoing Minnesota investigation.

actions.title: AmerisourceBergen Subsidiary Agrees to Pay $1 Million for Allegedly Paying Kickbacks
actions.description: A subsidiary of AmerisourceBergen agreed to pay $1 million to resolve allegations of paying unlawful inducements (kickbacks) to health care providers and practice executives. The government alleges that ASD caused physicians to submit false claims to Medicare and Medicaid induced by these kickbacks.

actions.title: Illinois Man Sentenced to Two Years in Prison for Durable Medical Equipment Fraud Scheme
actions.description: An Illinois man was sentenced to two years in federal prison for a fraud scheme to defraud Medicare of over $2 million by submitting claims for durable medical equipment (DME) that was medically unnecessary, not wanted by the Medicare beneficiaries and tainted by kickbacks.

actions.title: Kansas Doctor Sentenced to 3 Years in Prison for $8 Million Medicare Fraud
actions.description: A Kansas physician was sentenced to three years in federal prison for accepting hundreds of thousands of dollars in kickbacks to order medically unnecessary health care for thousands of patients. From 2017 to 2020, Dr. Roethle played a central role in a massive telemarketing scheme that billed Medicare for medically unnecessary orthotic braces. Overseas call centers collected Medicare information from unsuspecting elderly patients. Doctors, including Dr. Roethle, signed fraudulent orthotic brace prescriptions without evaluating patients or their actual medical needs and while falsely claiming to be their treating physician and having examined and diagnosed them. The brace suppliers mailed out the braces and billing companies then submitted the fraudulent claims to Medicare. Dr. Roethle admitted providing no follow-up care after signing the orders. Medicare paid out at least $8 million while relying on his fraudulent orders.

actions.title: Man Sentenced to 14 Years in Federal Prison for Leading a $6.85 Million Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Orlando Omar Garcia-Moya, 54, of Piedras Negras, was sentenced to 14 years in federal prison for leading a health care fraud conspiracy. In 2008, Garcia-Moya founded Fleet Ambulance Service Inc., an ambulance company operating in and around Brackettville, Texas. As part of a fraud conspiracy involving Garcia-Moya and two employees, Oscar Gutierrez and Melody Ann Villarreal, the conspirators fraudulently billed Medicare and Medicaid for services and received reimbursements they were not authorized to receive. The conspirators created fraudulent run sheets for patients who were not transported, transported patients in personal vehicles or vehicles not approved for appropriate medical transfers, and used ghost employees in the run sheets. From 2008 to 2012, Fleet Ambulance billed Medicare, Medicaid, and Managed Care Organizations (MCOs) more than $12.3 million; Medicare and Medicaid paid a total of $4,741,601.73. From January 1, 2013 to April 2016, Fleet Ambulance billed more than $5.6 million; Medicare and Medicaid paid a total of $2,142,491.12. The total amount paid by Medicare and Medicaid as a result of the fraud was $6,856,186.85.

actions.title: Over $12 Million Medicaid Fraud Scheme Leads to 14 Years of Prison for Substance Abuse Facility, Owner, Compliance Officer, and Office Manager
actions.description: A federal judge sentenced participants in a $12.7 million Medicaid fraud scheme to more than 14 years in federal prison. The scheme paid over $1 million in kickbacks to patients with substance abuse disorders and operated through a fake substance abuse treatment facility in New Bern, North Carolina. The case also resulted in the permanent closure of the facility, a $15 million fine, and seizure of more than $6 million in criminal proceeds including cash, cars, and homes.

actions.title: DOJ: Russian Citizen Charged with Laundering $1.2M Connected to $400M in Fraudulent Medicare Claims
actions.description: Nikolai Buzolin, 38, of Russia, charged with conspiracy to commit money laundering in connection with Verisola Inc., which submitted $400M+ in false Medicare Advantage claims for DME (orthotic braces, glucose monitors) never provided to patients. MAOs reimbursed at least $1.7M, of which at least $1.2M was wired overseas. Buzolin was arrested in LA attempting to flee to Moscow. Scheme ran Aug 2025-Jan 2026.

actions.title: House Oversight Hearing: Minnesota Fraud Part II — Governor Walz and AG Ellison Testify
actions.description: House Oversight Committee held second hearing on Minnesota Medicaid fraud with testimony from Gov. Tim Walz and AG Keith Ellison. Committee interim staff report alleged Walz and Ellison were aware of widespread fraud since 2019, misled the public, and retaliated against whistleblowers. Federal prosecutors estimate up to $9 billion may have been stolen from fourteen Medicaid programs administered by Minnesota.

actions.title: Dr. Oz Launches Medicaid Fraud Investigation of New York; Sends 50-Question Letter to Gov. Hochul
actions.description: CMS Administrator Dr. Mehmet Oz sent a letter to New York Gov. Kathy Hochul posing 50 detailed questions about fraud, waste, and abuse in New York's $124 billion Medicaid program—one of the largest state Medicaid programs in the country. Oz cited serious concerns about New York's oversight of personal care, home health, adult day care programming, non-emergency medical transportation, and behavioral health services, noting per-capita spending and workforce utilization patterns significantly exceeding national norms. Hochul was given 30 days to respond with a corrective action plan or face potential deferral of federal Medicaid payments. CMS cited the $68 million Brooklyn adult day care fraud as an example.

actions.title: Atlanta Gastroenterology Associates Pays $4.75M to Settle Kickbacks and Unnecessary Testing Allegations
actions.description: Atlanta Gastroenterology Associates (Atlanta, GA) agreed to pay $4.75 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that it received kickbacks from Advanced Pathology Solutions (APS, AR) in exchange for referrals of gastrointestinal pathology services, and performed certain gastrointestinal pathology services that were not medically reasonable or necessary through automatic reflex ordering without medical justification.
officials: Jonathan D. Ross (U.S. Attorney, E.D. Ark.)

actions.title: CMS Publishes CRUSH RFI in Federal Register; Seeks Public Input on Sweeping Anti-Fraud Regulation
actions.description: CMS published a Request for Information (RFI) in the Federal Register as part of its Comprehensive Regulations to Uncover Suspicious Health Care (CRUSH) initiative, signaling a forthcoming major anti-fraud rulemaking. The RFI covers over a dozen subject areas including enhanced provider enrollment screening, identity proofing, preclusion list reforms, laboratory test fraud, DMEPOS supplier oversight, claim filing deadlines, AI-assisted coding oversight, beneficiary solicitation protections, surety bonds, Medicaid and CHIP fraud, and Marketplace program integrity. Public comments due March 30, 2026.

actions.title: CMS/HHS Announce Major Anti-Fraud Package: $259.5M MN Deferral, DMEPOS Moratorium, CRUSH Initiative
actions.description: Vice President J.D. Vance, HHS Secretary RFK Jr., and CMS Administrator Dr. Oz jointly announced a sweeping anti-fraud package at the White House. Actions included: (1) $259.5 million deferral of quarterly federal Medicaid matching funds to Minnesota ($243.8M for fraudulent claims; $15.4M for unauthorized immigrant claims); (2) a nationwide six-month moratorium on new Medicare DMEPOS supplier enrollment, building on $1.5 billion in prevented DMEPOS billing in 2025; (3) the CRUSH (Comprehensive Regulations to Uncover Suspicious Health Care) initiative RFI published in the Federal Register; (4) CMS-State Tax Fraud Partnership with 28 states. CMS metrics for 2025: $5.7 billion suspended, 122,658 claims denied, 5,586 providers revoked, 372 fraud referrals totaling $3.7 billion. The administration announced a shift from a 'pay and chase' model to a real-time 'detect and deploy' strategy using AI tools to identify fraud before payments are issued.

actions.title: Texas Orthopedic Surgeon Dr. Michael Taba Sentenced to 8.5 Years for $145M DOL Workers' Comp Prescription Fraud
actions.description: Dr. Michael Taba, of McKinney, Texas, was sentenced to 102 months (8.5 years) in prison and ordered to pay over $13 million in restitution following his November 2023 conviction for conspiracy to commit health care fraud and three counts of health care fraud. Taba accepted bribes from pharmacy owners to prescribe medically unnecessary compound creams to injured federal workers. From May 2014 to March 2017, pharmacies billed the Department of Labor's Office of Workers' Compensation Programs and Blue Cross Blue Shield more than $145 million and received more than $90 million for Taba-referred prescriptions.

actions.title: Arizona OASIS Hospital/USPI Pay $5.6M to Resolve Improper Financial Arrangement with Physicians
actions.description: Southwest Orthopedic and Spine Hospital LLC (d/b/a OASIS Hospital), United Surgical Partners International Inc. (USPI), and an affiliated entity agreed to pay $5.6 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations involving improper financial relationships with a referring physician group, including alleged interest payments on convertible bonds, violating both the Anti-Kickback Statute and the Stark Law (Physician Self-Referral Law) from 2011–2018.
officials: Scott J. Lampert (HHS-OIG Acting Deputy IG)

actions.title: Former NFL Player Keith Gray Convicted in $328M Medicare Genetic Testing Fraud Scheme
actions.description: A federal jury in Dallas convicted Keith J. Gray, 39, of McKinney, Texas — a former NFL player and owner of Axis Professional Labs LLC and Kingdom Health Laboratory LLC — for his role in a $328 million cardiovascular genetic testing fraud scheme. Gray paid kickbacks to marketers for referrals of Medicare beneficiaries’ DNA samples, personal information, and signed test orders from medical providers authorizing medically unnecessary genetic tests. Axis and Kingdom billed Medicare approximately $328 million and Medicare paid approximately $54 million. Gray also laundered some of the proceeds through luxury vehicle purchases.

actions.title: Two Executives Sentenced to 20 Years Each for $233M ACA Enrollment Fraud Targeting Homeless and Vulnerable
actions.description: Cory Lloyd and Steven Strong were each sentenced to 20 years in prison for their roles in a years-long scheme to defraud the Affordable Care Act (ACA) program. Through street marketers, they targeted vulnerable individuals, including people experiencing homelessness and those facing mental health or substance abuse challenges, and fraudulently enrolled them in fully subsidized ACA plans to collect insurance commissions. The scheme sought over $233 million in fraudulent subsidies, of which the federal government paid at least $180 million.

actions.title: Zynex, Inc. Agrees to Non-Prosecution Agreement for Health Care, Securities, and Mail Fraud
actions.description: Zynex agreed to a non-prosecution agreement and admitted to conspiring to commit health care fraud, securities fraud, mail fraud, and related offenses. The company fraudulently billed government and private payors and patients for excessive and medically unnecessary devices and supplies, collected more than $873 million, and agreed to forfeit more than $98 million in unpaid claims.

actions.title: DOGE Releases Largest Medicaid Dataset in History to Crowdsource Fraud Detection
actions.description: DOGE released the largest Medicaid dataset in HHS history — 10 GB of provider-level claims data covering fee-for-service, managed care, and CHIP claims from 2018–2024 — to the public via opendata.hhs.gov. The dataset aggregates billing by provider, procedure code, and month, enabling public identification of anomalous billing patterns. DOGE cited Minnesota's large-scale autism diagnosis fraud as an example of what the data could surface. Elon Musk posted: "Medicaid data has been open sourced, so the level of fraud is easy to identify."

actions.title: DOJ: Two Foreign Nationals Indicted in Chicago as Part of $10M Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Burhan Mirza and Kashif Iqbal were indicted for participating in a $10 million scheme that used nominee-owned laboratories and durable medical equipment providers to bill Medicare and private insurers for items and services that were never provided. Mirza allegedly obtained identifying information without victims’ knowledge, while Iqbal allegedly managed DME providers, laundered fraud proceeds, and coordinated transfers to Pakistan.

actions.title: House Oversight Committee Investigates New York CDPAP Medicaid Fraud — $1.2B+ Lost to Scammers
actions.description: Chairman James Comer and the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform took formal action in its investigation of fraud in New York's Consumer Directed Personal Assistance Program (CDPAP), a Medicaid-funded home care program. Spending ballooned from $2.5 billion in 2019 to $9.1 billion by 2023, with an estimated $1.2 billion or more lost to fraud and abuse. A former 27-year Medicaid fraud prosecutor described it as 'Minnesota multiplied by 10.' Notable cases include Brooklyn businesswoman Zakia Khan, who admitted to a $68 million fraud billing for services never provided, and a Brooklyn CEO sentenced for a $100 million home health care scheme.

actions.title: HSGAC Subcommittee Hearing: Examining Fraud and Foreign Influence in State and Federal Programs
actions.description: Senate Homeland Security subcommittee examined foreign actors exploiting state and federal programs including Medicaid. Minnesota fraud used as a central case study.

actions.title: DOJ: 'Fraud Tourists' Plead Guilty to $3.5M Minneapolis Medicaid Fraud Using AI-Fabricated Records
actions.description: Anthony Waddell Jefferson and Lester Brown pleaded guilty to repeatedly traveling from Philadelphia to Minneapolis to defraud Minnesota’s Housing Stabilization Services program of approximately $3.5 million. They targeted vulnerable people at homeless shelters, billed for services never provided, and used AI to fabricate client records when questioned by insurers.

actions.title: House Judiciary Subpoenas 8 Major Health Insurers Over ACA Enrollment Fraud
actions.description: Subpoenas issued to Blue Shield of California, Centene, CVS Health, Elevance Health, GuideWell, HCSC, Kaiser Permanente, and Oscar Health demanding information on fraud-protection measures, enrollees with unused subsidies, broker payments, and internal fraud audits.

actions.title: Two Queens, NY Men Charged with $120M Adult Day Care and Pharmacy Medicare/Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: Inwoo Kim and Daniel Lee were charged with conspiracy to commit health care fraud for allegedly paying cash bribes and gift certificates to induce Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries to fill prescriptions and enroll in adult day care services. Prosecutors say the scheme generated claims for drugs and services that were medically unnecessary, never provided, or induced by kickbacks and bribes, costing Medicare and Medicaid approximately $120 million.
officials: Scott J. Lampert (HHS-OIG Acting Deputy IG)

actions.title: CMS Expands Enhanced Hospice Oversight to Georgia and Ohio
actions.description: CMS expanded its Provisional Period of Enhanced Oversight (PPEO) for newly enrolling hospices from four states (Arizona, California, Nevada, Texas) to include Georgia and Ohio. CMS Administrator Dr. Oz and agency officials visited hospice and home health fraud hot spots in Nevada and California. CMS reported receiving numerous reports of hospice fraud, waste, and abuse in Georgia and Ohio.
actions.state: AZ, CA, GA, NV, OH, TX

actions.title: Senate Judiciary Subcommittee Hearing: Somali Scammers — Fighting Fraud in Minnesota and Beyond
actions.description: Senate Judiciary Subcommittee on Federal Courts, Oversight, Agency Action, and Federal Rights held a hearing chaired by Sen. Ted Cruz titled "Somali Scammers: Fighting Fraud in Minnesota and Beyond." Witnesses included independent investigative journalist David Hoch, Public Citizen co-president Robert Weissman, and Minnesota state Rep. Kristin Robbins, chair of the state House Fraud Prevention Committee.

actions.title: House Energy & Commerce Hearing: Common Schemes, Real Harm — Examining Medicare/Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: House Energy & Commerce Oversight and Investigations Subcommittee hearing examining current trends in fraud schemes targeting Medicare and Medicaid and how transnational crime organizations are increasingly involved. Cases highlighted included a $68 million adult daycare Medicaid fraud scheme in New York, a $650 million substance abuse treatment fraud in Arizona targeting homeless individuals and Native Americans, a $1.1 billion Medicare fraud scheme involving medically unnecessary amniotic wound grafts across Arizona and Nevada, and a $52 million genetic testing fraud in Florida. Chairman Joyce cited estimates of $100 billion in annual losses from detected fraud alone.

actions.title: Consolidated Appropriations Act 2026 Signed: PBM Transparency Reform and Anti-Fraud Provisions
actions.description: H.R. 7148 signed into law with major pharmacy benefit manager (PBM) reform. Beginning 2028, PBMs prohibited from deriving remuneration from rebates, spread pricing, or volume-based arrangements; only flat-fee bona fide service fees permitted. Mandates semiannual PBM reporting on drug spending and rebates. Makes it unlawful to submit false or misleading pharmacy benefit information to federal agencies. PBMs liable for civil monetary penalties. Prohibits reimbursement clawbacks unless original claim was fraudulent. $321M+ earmarked for drug cost transparency.

actions.title: Former NFL Player Joel French Convicted in $197M Medicare Fraud Scheme Targeting Seniors
actions.description: A federal jury convicted Joel Rufus French, 47, of Amory, Mississippi — a former NFL player who played for the Green Bay Packers and Seattle Seahawks — for his role in a scheme to fraudulently bill Medicare and CHAMPVA approximately $197 million for medically unnecessary orthotic braces. French worked with overseas call centers that pressured elderly Americans, including those with Alzheimer's disease and dementia, to provide personal health information and accept unwanted braces. Call center operators altered recordings to falsely document patient consent. French paid sham telemedicine companies to obtain signed orders from doctors and nurse practitioners who never examined the patients, then sold those orders to medical supply companies that submitted false Medicare claims — including for amputees and deceased beneficiaries. French was convicted of conspiracy to commit health care fraud and wire fraud, conspiracy to commit money laundering, and conspiracy to pay and receive kickbacks.

actions.title: Massachusetts Sleep Doctor Dr. Pankaj Merchia Convicted of Health Care Fraud, Money Laundering, and Tax Evasion
actions.description: A federal jury in Boston convicted Dr. Pankaj Merchia, a Harvard-educated sleep medicine physician, for health care fraud, three counts of money laundering, conspiracy to defraud the IRS, and two counts of tax evasion. Merchia fraudulently billed insurance companies millions of dollars for sleep apnea machines not used by patients for years (2017—2019), billed over $390,000 for fraudulent family member treatment claims, and failed to report over $6.5 million in income (2009—2019) from his medical businesses. He purchased a $2.1 million Massachusetts home with fraud proceeds. Sentencing scheduled for April 28, 2026.

actions.title: CMS Issues Final Rule Closing Medicaid Provider Tax Loophole; $78.2B Decade Savings
actions.description: CMS finalized the 'Preserving Medicaid Funding for Vulnerable Populations—Closing a Health Care-Related Tax Loophole Final Rule,' codifying the Medicaid provider tax provisions adopted by Congress in the Working Families Tax Cuts legislation (Public Law 119-21, signed July 4, 2025). The rule prohibits states from taxing Medicaid business at higher rates than non-Medicaid, blocks indirect tax structures disguising disproportionate Medicaid burdens, and strengthens CMS enforcement authority. CMS estimates the rule saves the federal government $78.2 billion over the next decade, addressing financing gimmicks that drew down over $24 billion annually. Effective April 3, 2026, with phased state compliance deadlines through FY2028.

actions.title: Dr. Oz Calls on Governor Newsom to Produce Comprehensive CA Anti-Fraud Action Plan
actions.description: Dr. Oz publicly called on California Governor Gavin Newsom to produce a comprehensive action plan to address major fraud in the California health care system.

actions.title: Senate HELP Committee Launches Task Force to Eliminate Fraud in Federal Spending
actions.description: Senate HELP Committee Chair Bill Cassidy launched Republican task force to eliminate fraud in federal spending, including health care. Health prong led by Sens. Ashley Moody (FL) and Roger Marshall (KS). Task force formed in response to Minnesota fraud scandal; estimates up to 10% of the U.S. budget is stolen annually by fraudsters.

actions.title: Florida Nursing Assistant Convicted: $11.4M Medicare Fraud
actions.description: A Florida nursing assistant was convicted for his or her role in an $11.4 million health care fraud scheme targeting Medicare beneficiaries. The conviction, secured by the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of Florida, reflects ongoing DOJ efforts to hold individual health care workers accountable for Medicare fraud, not just corporate entities.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Maine Audit: $45.6M Improper Medicaid Payments for Autism Services
actions.description: HHS-OIG reported its Maine audit revealed Maine made at least $45.6 million in improper Medicaid payments for autism services for children. This is part of a series of audits looking into unallowable services and questionable billing patterns in Medicaid ABA services provided to children diagnosed with autism. To date, HHS-OIG has completed three state audits: Indiana, Wisconsin, and Maine.

actions.title: House E&C and Ways & Means: Health Insurance CEO Hearings on Costs, MA Upcoding, and Vertical Integration
actions.description: Back-to-back hearings before House Energy & Commerce and Ways & Means committees with CEOs of UnitedHealth Group, CVS Health, Elevance Health, Cigna Group, and Blue Shield. Bipartisan questioning on rising premiums, prior authorization denials, vertical integration, and Medicare Advantage upcoding. Chair Jason Smith questioned extent of marketplace consolidation.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Fall 2025 Semiannual Report: $19.04B in Monetary Impact (Apr—Sep 2025)
actions.description: HHS-OIG's Fall 2025 Semiannual Report to Congress identified $19.04 billion in monetary impact for the period April 1 through September 30, 2025—reflecting a significant increase from the Spring 2025 report's $16.6 billion. The period covered the 2025 National Health Care Fraud Takedown (June 30, 2025) and related enforcement actions. The report covers the peak enforcement period including the $14.6 billion takedown, the $10.6 billion Operation Gold Rush EDNY indictment, and the launch of the Health Care Fraud Data Fusion Center.

actions.title: House E&C Launches Investigation into Ongoing Medicaid Fraud in Minnesota
actions.description: House Energy & Commerce Committee leaders launched a formal investigation into ongoing Medicaid fraud in Minnesota, requesting documents from Governor Walz and the Department of Human Services. The fraud scheme, potentially operating since 2013, involves overbilling, false records, identity theft, and phantom claims.

actions.title: DOJ Announces Record $6.8B in False Claims Act Recoveries in FY2025; Health Care Accounts for $5.7B
actions.description: DOJ announced that False Claims Act settlements and judgments exceeded $6.8 billion in FY2025 (ending September 30, 2025)—the highest single-year total in FCA history. Over $5.7 billion of that total involved the health care industry, restoring funds to Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE. Whistleblowers filed 1,297 qui tam lawsuits in FY2025 (a new record, surpassing 2024's 980), recovering over $5.3 billion. Since 1986, cumulative FCA recoveries have exceeded $85 billion.

actions.title: Brooklyn Defendants Plead Guilty: $68M Adult Day Care Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: Multiple Brooklyn defendants pleaded guilty in connection with a $68 million adult day care Medicaid fraud scheme. The case involved adult day care centers in Brooklyn billing New York Medicaid for services never provided to beneficiaries. This case was later cited by CMS Administrator Dr. Oz as a prominent example when he launched a 50-question investigation into New York's $124 billion Medicaid program in March 2026, noting patterns of fraud in adult day care programming that significantly exceeded national norms. Prosecuted by the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Eastern District of New York.

actions.title: CMS FY2025 Improper Payments Fact Sheet: $28.8B Medicare FFS, $23.7B Medicare Advantage
actions.description: CMS published its FY2025 Improper Payments Fact Sheet estimating Medicare Fee-for-Service improper payments at $28.83 billion (6.55% rate), down from $31.70 billion in FY2024. Medicare Advantage (Part C) improper payments rose to $23.67 billion (6.09% rate), up from $19.07 billion, primarily driven by unsupported diagnoses submitted by MA organizations. Medicaid improper payments were $37.39 billion (6.12% rate). CHIP improper payments were $1.37 billion (7.05% rate). CMS noted that most improper payments reflect documentation issues rather than fraud per se.

actions.title: Five Florida Ophthalmology Practices Pay $6M to Resolve Fraudulent TCD Ultrasound Kickback Claims
actions.description: Five Florida ophthalmology practices—Clay Eye Holdings LLC, Retina Macula Specialist of Miami LLC, Florida Eye Institute P.A., Miami Eye LLC, and Kendall Eye Institute Inc.—agreed to pay nearly $6 million total to resolve False Claims Act allegations of billing Medicare and Medicaid for medically unnecessary trans-cranial Doppler ultrasound (TCD) tests through a kickback arrangement with a third-party testing company. Practices performed TCDs on thousands of patients and pre-labeled them with serious diagnoses (that nearly all patients did not have) before tests were complete. The State of Florida received $333,500 for its Medicaid share; the whistleblower received $1.135 million.
officials: Gregory W. Kehoe (U.S. Attorney, M.D. Fla.)

actions.title: CMS Withholds $300M from California Over Unlawful Health Care Spending on Illegal Immigrants
actions.description: Dr. Oz reported CMS will withhold nearly $300 million in Medicaid funding from California because of the scale of its unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants. CMS found more than $1.8 billion in unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants across 8 states.

actions.title: Kaiser Permanente Affiliates Pay $556M to Resolve Medicare Advantage False Claims Act Allegations
actions.description: Kaiser Foundation Health Plan Inc. and affiliated Kaiser entities in California and Colorado agreed to pay $556 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that Kaiser systematically pressured physicians to alter patient medical records after visits by adding diagnoses not considered or addressed during those visits—mining patients' histories and sending physician 'queries' months or over a year after visits to generate addenda for unsupported diagnoses, and linking physician bonuses to meeting diagnosis goals. This is one of the largest Medicare Advantage risk-adjustment fraud settlements in history.
officials: Scott J. Lampert (HHS-OIG Acting Deputy IG)

actions.title: House Oversight Hearing: Curbing Federal Fraud — Examining Innovative Detection Tools
actions.description: House Oversight Subcommittee on Government Operations examined innovative tools to detect and prevent fraud in federal programs. GAO estimates the federal government loses $231-521 billion annually to fraud. Witnesses included PRAC Executive Director and GAO Chief Scientist. Discussion of transitioning pandemic fraud detection infrastructure to government-wide use.

actions.title: Senate Report: UnitedHealth 'Aggressively' Gaming Medicare Advantage, Turning Risk Adjustment Into Profit Engine
actions.description: Sen. Grassley's 105-page Senate Judiciary Committee report, based on 50,000+ pages of internal UnitedHealth documents, finds the nation's largest MA insurer (10M members) systematically transformed diagnosis coding into a 'major profit-centered strategy.' UnitedHealth guides providers to apply lucrative diagnosis codes, including diagnosing patients taking prescribed opioids as physically dependent. DOJ has opened both criminal and civil fraud investigations into UnitedHealth's billing practices.

actions.title: Dr. Oz and Deputy Secretary O'Neill Fact-Finding Mission to Minnesota
actions.description: Dr. Oz and HHS Deputy Secretary Jim O'Neill conducted a fact-finding mission to Minnesota, signaling heightened federal oversight of the state's Medicaid program. O'Neill posted publicly about their findings, emphasizing the administration's focus on uncovering fraud.

actions.title: White House Announces New DOJ National Fraud Enforcement Division; Health Care Fraud a Core Focus
actions.description: President Trump established a new Department of Justice Division for National Fraud Enforcement to coordinate criminal and civil fraud enforcement against federal programs including Medicare, Medicaid, and other HHS-funded programs. The division is led by an Assistant Attorney General appointed by the President. In January 2026, President Trump nominated Colin McDonald as the first AAG of the new division.

actions.title: DOJ: Oklahoma Medical Supply Company Owner Indicted for $30M Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Mark Loftis, 38, of Cushing, OK, owner of Back Pain Home Supplies LLC (EZ Medical Supply), indicted for paying illegal kickbacks to marketers and telemedicine companies for fraudulent DME orders. Submitted approximately $30 million in false claims; federal programs paid approximately $8 million. Also misused more than $133,000 in COVID-19 Provider Relief Funds for personal expenses.

actions.title: House Oversight Hearing: Oversight of Fraud and Misuse of Federal Funds in Minnesota — Part I
actions.description: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform held hearing on Minnesota Medicaid fraud. Federal prosecutors estimate up to $9 billion stolen from 14 Minnesota Medicaid programs. Witnesses included Minnesota state legislators from the Fraud Prevention Committee. Feeding Our Future scandal alone involved ~$300 million stolen. Fraud identified in autism centers, sober homes, transportation, adult day services, and assisted living.

actions.title: Dr. Oz Announces Plans to Audit Minnesota Medicaid; Defer High-Risk Payments
actions.description: CMS Administrator Dr. Oz announced plans to audit Minnesota's Medicaid program and defer payments to high-risk programs, following the massive Minnesota Medicaid fraud scandal. CMS identified approximately 400 Medicaid-billed businesses operating out of a single building that generated approximately $380 million in taxpayer billing.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: Medicaid Agencies Made $207.5M in Unallowable Capitation Payments for Deceased Enrollees
actions.description: HHS-OIG Report A-04-23-09010 estimated that Medicaid agencies made $207.5 million ($138.6 million federal share) in unallowable capitation payments to MCOs for enrollees whose deaths preceded the monthly service periods during FY2022 (July 2021—June 2022). In a sample of 100 payments, 99 were unallowable—49 already not recovered or recovered only after OIG notification. This represents OIG's 18th such audit since 2016, which cumulatively identified approximately $289 million in improper capitation payments for deceased enrollees. OIG recommended CMS provide matched T-MSIS data to states for recovery.

actions.title: House Oversight Widens Investigation into Minnesota Social Services and Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: Chairman Comer widened the investigation into extensive money laundering and fraud in Minnesota's social services programs, following findings by the U.S. Attorney for the District of Minnesota. A legislative audit revealed a grantee receiving nearly $680,000 for a single month without documentation.

actions.title: DMERx CEO Gary Cox Sentenced to 15 Years in Prison; $452M Restitution for $1B Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Gary Cox, 79, of Maricopa County, Arizona, CEO of Power Mobility Doctor Rx LLC (DMERx), was sentenced to 15 years in prison and ordered to pay over $452 million in restitution following his June 2025 conviction for conspiring to defraud Medicare of more than $1 billion. The FBI, HHS-OIG, VA-OIG, and DCIS investigated the case. This represents one of the longest individual sentences for Medicare fraud in recent years.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: Illinois Made $9.5M in Unallowable Medicaid Capitation Payments for Incarcerated Enrollees
actions.description: HHS-OIG Report A-05-24-00019 found Illinois made unallowable capitation payments to managed care organizations for incarcerated Medicaid enrollees who were ineligible for coverage. In a sample of 100 incarcerated enrollees, 48 had unallowable payments totaling $263,186. Extrapolated to the audit period, OIG estimated at least $9.5 million ($8.3 million federal share) in unallowable payments. OIG recommended Illinois refund $8,366,521 in federal funds and expand its automated enrollment termination process to cover inmates outside Illinois DOC facilities. Illinois accepted both recommendations.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Audit: Mississippi Failed to Report $4.5M in MFCU Medicaid Overpayments
actions.description: Federal audit found Mississippi failed to report and return MFCU-determined Medicaid overpayments totaling $4.5 million ($3.7M federal share) across 20 cases in FY 2021-2023. OIG recommended Mississippi return $3.5M in federal share.

actions.title: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute Pays $15M to Settle NIH Research Grant Fraud Allegations
actions.description: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute agreed to pay $15 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that from 2014—2024 it made materially false statements and certifications related to NIH research grants. Specifically, researchers authored papers containing duplicated and manipulated images/data from 14 scientific publications, with six NIH grants funding the fraudulent research and four additional NIH grants obtained through false applications. Welsh molecular biologist and whistleblower Sholto David received $2.625 million under qui tam provisions. U.S. Attorney Leah B. Foley noted 'there is no place in scientific research for fraud, waste and abuse.'
officials: Leah B. Foley (U.S. Attorney, D. Mass.)

actions.title: HHS-OIG Medicare Advantage Audit of Humana Louisiana Finds .5M in Unsupported Diagnosis Overpayments
actions.description: HHS-OIG published a compliance audit of Humana Health Benefit of Louisiana (Contract H1951) for Medicare Advantage risk adjustment payments for 2017-2018. The audit found that most selected diagnosis codes did not comply with Federal requirements, estimating Humana was overpaid approximately $10.5 million for the audited contract when extrapolated, with OIG recommending Humana refund $5,470,725 to the Federal Government. Most unsupported diagnoses resulted from past medical conditions improperly reported as current conditions, or from radiology/ECG reports not interpreted and signed by an acceptable provider type.

actions.title: House Judiciary Launches Probe of 8 Major Insurers Over ACA Enrollment Fraud
actions.description: House Judiciary Committee sent demand letters to Blue Shield of California, Centene, CVS Health/Aetna, Elevance Health, GuideWell, Health Care Service Corporation, Kaiser Permanente, and Oscar Health demanding documents about ACA subsidy fraud and unauthorized broker enrollment practices.

actions.title: House Judiciary Joint Hearing: Fighting Obamacare Subsidy Fraud — Is the APA Working as Intended?
actions.description: Joint hearing of House Judiciary Subcommittees on Oversight and Administrative State examined ACA subsidy fraud following the GAO report finding 96% of fictitious applications were approved. Witnesses included Dr. Ge Bai (Johns Hopkins) and Seto Bagdoyan (GAO). Examined how enhanced COVID-era subsidies created an environment for waste, fraud, and abuse.

actions.title: Virginia Laboratory NEXT Molecular Analytics Pays $758K+ to Settle Medicare Kickback Allegations
actions.description: NEXT Bio-Research Services LLC (d/b/a NEXT Molecular Analytics), a Chester, Virginia clinical laboratory, agreed to pay at least $758,000 plus contingent amounts to resolve False Claims Act allegations of paying illegal kickbacks to physicians and independent contractor marketers disguised as consulting fees and medical director fees—paid on a per-referral basis—to induce laboratory testing referrals. The kickback scheme involved doctors in Texas and Arkansas.

actions.title: GAO Report: 96% of Fictitious ACA Marketplace Applications Approved; $94M+ in Subsidies Tied to Deceased
actions.description: GAO created 20 fictitious identities and submitted 24 applications; 23 of 24 were approved. 18 of 20 fictitious enrollees remained actively covered. Found nearly 68,000 SSNs in plan year 2024 used to receive more than one year of coverage with APTC. Over 58,000 SSNs receiving subsidies matched death records in plan year 2023 ($94M+ in APTC tied to households with deceased members). Total APTC paid was nearly $124 billion for 19.5 million enrollees in plan year 2024.

actions.title: Georgia Man Patrick Moore Sentenced to 46 Months for $24M Medicare Genetic Testing Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Patrick C. Moore Jr., of Peachtree City, Georgia, was sentenced to 46 months in prison and ordered to pay $7.2 million in restitution for his role orchestrating a network of recruiters targeting Medicare beneficiaries for medically unnecessary genetic tests. Moore billed approximately $24 million to Medicare and received approximately $4.3 million in illegal kickbacks. He pleaded guilty in May 2025 to one count of conspiracy to defraud the United States and to pay and receive illegal health care kickbacks.

actions.title: Washington Sleep Clinic Owner Pleads Guilty to Selling Adulterated CPAP Devices
actions.description: A physician and owner of a Washington state sleep clinic pleaded guilty to adulterating and misbranding medical devices. He purchased at least 500 used and recalled Philips CPAP/BiPAP machines, directed staff to remove defective foam, and distributed them to patients.

actions.title: Vohra Wound Physicians Pays $45M to Settle Medicare Overbilling Fraud Allegations
actions.description: Dr. Ameet Vohra and Vohra Wound Physicians Management LLC agreed to pay $45 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that the company billed Medicare for surgical excisional debridement procedures that were either not medically necessary or not performed. The company's proprietary EMR and billing software was programmed to automatically bill every debridement as the higher-reimbursed surgical procedure and to auto-populate clinical documentation to falsely support surgical billing, regardless of what was actually done. Physicians were pressured and financially incentivized to perform procedures at every visit. Vohra is one of the nation's largest providers of bedside wound care in nursing homes and skilled nursing facilities. The case settled approximately seven months after DOJ filed the complaint in April 2025. Vohra also entered a five-year Corporate Integrity Agreement with HHS-OIG.

actions.title: Four California Sham Hospice Operators Sentenced in $16M Medicare Fraud and Money Laundering Scheme
actions.description: Four California defendants were sentenced for their roles in operating sham hospice companies that billed Medicare nearly $16 million for medically unnecessary and never-provided hospice services from July 2019 to January 2023. Sentences: Juan Carlos Esparza (57 months), Karpis Srapyan (57 months), Mihran Panosyan (57 months), and Petros Fichidzhyan (12 years—sentenced separately in April 2025). The scheme used foreign nationals' identities as purported hospice owners and laundered proceeds through shell companies and bank accounts.

actions.title: Done Telehealth CEO and Clinical President Convicted in $100M Adderall Distribution and Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: A federal jury in San Francisco convicted Ruthia He, founder and CEO of Done (California digital health company), and David Brody, its clinical president, for illegally distributing over 40 million Adderall pills online and conspiring to commit health care fraud. The defendants spent $40 million on deceptive social media advertising to attract drug seekers, defrauded insurers of approximately $14 million through false prior authorization requests, and He relocated Done to China to obstruct the DOJ investigation and destroy incriminating documents. He was also convicted of conspiracy to obstruct justice. Sentencing was scheduled for February 25, 2026.

actions.title: Atrium Health and Senior Living (KBWB) and CEO Sentenced for $146M Health Care Fraud and Tax Conspiracy
actions.description: KBWB Operations LLC (d/b/a Atrium Health and Senior Living) and its former CEO/managing member Kevin Breslin, 58, of Hoboken, NJ, were sentenced following December 2024 guilty pleas. Breslin received 90 months in prison; both Breslin and KBWB were ordered to pay $146 million in restitution and $8.4 million in forfeiture. The defendants diverted CMS funds intended for resident care at 23 skilled nursing facilities in New Jersey, Wisconsin, and Michigan—prioritizing owner distributions, diverting employee health insurance premiums and 401(k) contributions, and failing to remit employment taxes to the IRS.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: CMS Put $11.2 Billion at Risk by Not Properly Closing Federal Contracts
actions.description: HHS-OIG Report A-04-24-02048 found that CMS failed to meet administrative closeout requirements for 50 contracts totaling $11.2 billion reviewed for the period October 2018—September 2023. Twelve contracts worth $2.1 billion remained overdue for closeout at audit conclusion, putting those funds at risk of fraud, waste, and abuse. OIG recommended CMS complete administrative closeout for the 12 delinquent contracts and develop clear policies defining documentation requirements and roles for timely closure. CMS concurred with both recommendations.

actions.title: Co-Conspirator Pleads Guilty in $54M California Radiology and Hospice Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Sophia Shaklian, 38, of Los Angeles, pleaded guilty to her role in a $54 million Medicare fraud scheme involving seven health care providers—including radiology imaging centers and a hospice—across Los Angeles County from 2019—2024, billing for services never performed. Shaklian managed and submitted false claims for the providers. Co-conspirator Alex Alexsanian, 48, of Burbank, pleaded guilty to money laundering conspiracy on January 20, 2026, and agreed to forfeit $3 million. The pair pocketed $23 million in total, laundering proceeds through a fake identity and using funds to buy $6 million in gold bars.

actions.title: Florida ACA Enrollment Fraud — $233M in Fraudulent Claims
actions.description: DOJ resolved major cases involving fraudulent ACA enrollment schemes in Florida, in which brokers and marketing firms submitted false applications to obtain premium tax credits and subsidies. The cases involved more than $233 million in fraudulent claims, with at least $180 million paid out by the government.

actions.title: CMS Restructures Skin Substitute Payments, Cutting an Estimated $19.6 Billion in Medicare Spending
actions.description: CMS reclassified skin substitute products from separately billable biologicals to incident-to supplies under the Physician Fee Schedule, reducing Medicare reimbursement by approximately 90%. CMS projects the change will reduce gross fee-for-service spending by $19.6 billion in 2026 alone, citing the program's growth from roughly $256 million in 2019 to over $10 billion in 2024 and the incentives for overutilization created by prior pricing.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Audit: Medicare Improperly Paid $22.7M to DME Suppliers for Items Provided During Inpatient Stays
actions.description: HHS-OIG audit found that Medicare improperly paid suppliers $22.7 million over seven years (2018–2024) for durable medical equipment, prosthetics, orthotics, and supplies (DMEPOS) provided to enrollees during inpatient hospital stays — payments that should not have been made because Medicare does not separately pay for DMEPOS during inpatient stays. Even after CMS implemented system edits in January 2020 intended to prevent such payments, $4.5 million in improper payments continued. OIG recommended recovery of up to $22.7 million from suppliers and refunds of up to $5.9 million to enrollees. CMS concurred with most recommendations.

actions.title: Queens, NY Doctor Alexander Baldonado Sentenced to 7 Years for $24M Medicare Fraud Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Dr. Alexander Baldonado, M.D., 69, of Queens, New York, was sentenced to seven years in prison and ordered to pay $2.21 million in restitution following his conviction at a five-day jury trial in February 2025. Baldonado received illegal cash kickbacks and bribes in exchange for ordering medically unnecessary laboratory tests (including cancer genetic tests) and orthotic braces, submitting over $24 million in fraudulent Medicare claims. Medicare actually paid approximately $2.2 million on these claims.

actions.title: DOJ Expands Health Care Fraud Strike Force to Massachusetts (Boston)
actions.description: The DOJ's Criminal Division announced the expansion of the Health Care Fraud Unit's New England Strike Force into the District of Massachusetts, embedding federal law enforcement—FBI, HHS-OIG, FDA, DEA, HSI, VA-OIG, and IRS-CI—alongside the existing U.S. Attorney's Office Health Care Fraud Unit in Boston. The expansion was driven by Massachusetts and Boston's status as a national hub for health care and life sciences, with particular focus on pharmaceutical/medical device company fraud, provider misconduct, and complex schemes targeting Medicare/Medicaid. In FY2025 alone, Massachusetts recovered over $450 million in coordinated actions.
officials: Matthew R. Galeotti (Acting AAG, Criminal Division)
Leah B. Foley (U.S. Attorney, D. Mass.)

actions.title: KFF Health News: Exactech Pays $8M to Settle FCA Allegations Over Defective Knee Implants Sold to Medicare/VA
actions.description: Gainesville, FL-based Exactech agreed to pay $8 million to settle False Claims Act allegations that it knowingly sold defective knee replacement components to Medicare, Medicaid, and VA patients. The company knew by 2008 that its Optetrak knee system failed prematurely at unacceptable rates but continued selling through 2018. Over 2,500 patients alleging injuries filed claims; many needed revision surgeries within three years. Case brought by whistleblower former sales agents and a surgeon.

actions.title: True Health Diagnostics CEO, Physicians, and Marketers Pay $6M+ to Settle Lab Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Christopher Grottenthaler, former CEO of True Health Diagnostics, Dr. Hong Davis, Dr. Elizabeth Seymour, and seven marketers agreed to pay a combined $6,068,462 to resolve False Claims Act allegations of a health care kickback scheme. Defendants allegedly offered and paid doctors kickbacks disguised as MSO (Management Service Organization) distributions and consulting fees to induce laboratory testing referrals, submitting false claims to Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE from January 2015 to January 2018. The Eastern District of Texas prosecuted the case.

actions.title: Senate Finance Hearing: The President's 2026 Health Care Agenda — HHS Secretary Kennedy Testifies
actions.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. testified before Senate Finance on the President's 2026 health care agenda. Chairman Crapo raised fraud prevention in his opening remarks, referencing CMS's discovery of 2.8 million duplicate Medicaid/ACA enrollments. Fraud was one of several topics discussed alongside vaccines, CDC leadership, and broader health policy.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: Medicare Skin Substitute Spending Surpasses $10 Billion Annually, Flags Major Fraud Risk
actions.description: HHS-OIG found Medicare Part B expenditures for skin substitutes surpassed $10 billion annually by end of 2024, raising major concerns about fraud, waste, and abuse. Costs for enrollees treated at home were four times higher than office-based settings. OIG flagged the program's vulnerability to questionable billing schemes, driven by manufacturers' rapid product introductions and spread pricing incentives that encourage providers to select higher-cost products.

actions.title: Kimberly-Clark Pays $40.4M for Selling Adulterated MicroCool Surgical Gowns
actions.description: Kimberly-Clark agreed to pay up to $40.4 million via a deferred prosecution agreement for selling millions of adulterated surgical gowns. An employee conducted fraudulent testing to avoid submitting a new 510(k) premarket notification to FDA.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: Medicare Remote Patient Monitoring Payments Exceed $500M; Flags Suspicious Billing Patterns
actions.description: HHS-OIG found Medicare payments for remote patient monitoring exceeded $500 million in 2024. OIG identified billing patterns warranting scrutiny: practices billing for a high proportion of patients with no prior history with the practice, and practices billing for multiple monitoring devices per patient per month. OIG noted that monitoring billing patterns can help CMS identify suspicious activity and protect program integrity.

actions.title: CMS Launches Nationwide Push to Remove Ineligible Medicaid Enrollees and Uphold Citizenship Requirements
actions.description: CMS launched a nationwide initiative directing all states to verify the citizenship and immigration status of Medicaid and CHIP enrollees through the Department of Homeland Security's SAVE program. CMS began sending states monthly enrollment reports identifying individuals whose status could not be confirmed through federal databases. HHS Secretary RFK Jr. stated: "Medicaid is a lifeline for vulnerable Americans — and I will protect it from abuse."

actions.title: CMS Notifies 103,000 Medicare Beneficiaries of Account Fraud Scheme Using Stolen Identities
actions.description: CMS identified suspicious unauthorized creation of Medicare.gov accounts using personal information obtained from unknown external sources. Approximately 103,000 beneficiaries were potentially impacted. Fraudsters used stolen identity data to create beneficiary online accounts, potentially accessing personal health information and Medicare benefits.

actions.title: Troy Health Enters First-Ever Health Care Non-Prosecution Agreement; AI Tool Used to Commit Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Troy Health, Inc. (d/b/a Troy Medicare), a North Carolina-based Medicare Advantage plan, entered into an 18-month Non-Prosecution Agreement (NPA) and agreed to pay $1.43 million—the first NPA under DOJ's updated Corporate Enforcement Policy and the first health care resolution involving misconduct facilitated by artificial intelligence. Troy misused its proprietary AI platform 'Troy.ai' to offer kickbacks to pharmacies for Medicare beneficiary data and to batch-enroll beneficiaries without consent. At the scheme's height, Troy enrolled over 300 beneficiaries in a single day approximately one minute apart.
officials: Matthew R. Galeotti (Acting AAG, Criminal Division)

actions.title: CVS Caremark Ordered to Pay $290M for Defrauding Medicare Part D Program
actions.description: A federal court ordered CVS Caremark to pay nearly $290 million for deliberately manipulating prescription drug cost data to overcharge Medicare Part D. Originated from whistleblower Sarah Behnke, former Aetna actuary.

actions.title: Louisiana Nurse Practitioner Convicted in $12.1M Medicare Genetic Testing Fraud Scheme
actions.description: A federal jury convicted Scharmaine Lawson Baker, 58, of Richmond, Texas, on six counts of health care fraud. From 2018 to 2019, Baker worked as an independent contractor for a Utah-based telehealth company and signed hundreds of orders for medically unnecessary cancer genetic tests for patients she never met or examined. Phone consultations typically lasted less than 60 seconds. Baker falsely diagnosed patients to justify the tests, including diagnosing male patients with cervical cancer. She caused over $12.1 million in fraudulent Medicare claims and received kickbacks from the telehealth company, which she later failed to disclose in her bankruptcy petition. Sentencing was scheduled for November 19, 2025.

actions.title: CMS and DOGE Find 2.8 Million Americans in Duplicate Medicaid/ACA Enrollments; $14B Annual Savings Identified
actions.description: CMS, in coordination with DOGE, announced the identification of 2.8 million Americans potentially enrolled in duplicate Medicaid/CHIP plans or simultaneously in both Medicaid/CHIP and subsidized ACA Exchange coverage: 1.2 million/month enrolled in Medicaid/CHIP across two or more states and 1.6 million/month enrolled in both Medicaid/CHIP and subsidized Exchange plans. CMS estimated $14 billion in annual taxpayer savings from eliminating duplicate enrollment. CMS directed states to recheck eligibility and provided 30-day notice to affected individuals. HHS Secretary RFK Jr. stated HHS staff 'uncovered millions who were illegally or improperly enrolled.'

actions.title: CVS Omnicare Ordered to Pay $949M for Dispensing Drugs Without Valid Prescriptions
actions.description: A federal judge ordered CVS Omnicare to pay $948.8 million for dispensing drugs to elderly and disabled people in long-term care facilities without valid prescriptions. Omnicare filed over 3.3 million false claims between 2010 and 2018.

actions.title: One Big Beautiful Bill Act Signed Into Law: $185B in Health Care Fraud Savings, Medicaid/ACA Overhaul
actions.description: H.R. 1 signed into law with sweeping health care fraud and program integrity provisions. Requires full ACA eligibility verification before premium tax credits; eliminates exploited special enrollment periods (CBO: 2.3M improper enrollees). Medicaid work requirements (80 hrs/month), six-month redeterminations, quarterly SSA Death Master File checks, cross-state duplicate enrollment prevention, and 3% error rate penalty for states. Invests $25M in AI fraud detection for Medicare. CBO estimates $185B in savings from fraud reforms; $900B+ in Medicaid changes over 10 years.

actions.title: DOJ and HHS Launch Joint False Claims Act Working Group with Six Health Care Enforcement Priorities
actions.description: DOJ and HHS jointly announced the creation of the DOJ-HHS False Claims Act Working Group, co-led by DOJ's Civil Division and the HHS General Counsel, CMS Center for Program Integrity, and HHS-OIG counsel. The group established six priority enforcement areas: Medicare Advantage upcoding, drug/device pricing fraud, patient access barriers, kickback schemes, defective medical devices, and EHR manipulation driving unnecessary utilization. The working group coordinates referrals from HHS to DOJ, cross-agency investigations, and maximizes recoveries.

actions.title: House Oversight Launches Investigation of Fraud in New York's Medicaid Programs
actions.description: Chairman Comer launched an investigation into allegations that New York State misrepresented federal Medicaid fund sources involving Nassau University Medical Center. The alleged scheme has reportedly cost taxpayers over $1 billion over 20+ years. Comer requested a staff briefing from CMS Administrator Dr. Oz.

actions.title: Florida DME Owner Peter Roussonicolos Sentenced to 12 Years for $61.5M Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Peter Roussonicolos, of Port Saint Lucie, Florida, was sentenced to 12 years in prison and 3 years supervised release for conspiring to defraud Medicare through five durable medical equipment supplier companies. Roussonicolos operated the companies as a silent partner while concealing his involvement due to prior felony convictions. The companies submitted approximately $61.5 million in false and fraudulent Medicare claims for medically unnecessary DME and were paid approximately $26.7 million. The scheme involved falsified enrollment forms and kickbacks to patient recruiters.

actions.title: Largest-Ever National Health Care Fraud Takedown
actions.description: DOJ charged 324 defendants nationwide for alleged participation in health care fraud schemes targeting Medicare, Medicaid, and other federal programs. Schemes involved more than $14.6 billion in intended losses. CMS reportedly prevented more than $4 billion in fraudulent payments as a result of these efforts.

actions.title: Operation Gold Rush — $10.6 Billion Medicare Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Largest case amount ever charged by DOJ. Members of a transnational criminal organization orchestrated a Medicare fraud scheme totaling more than $10.6 billion. The criminals purchased dozens of medical supply companies in the U.S. and submitted more than $10 billion in fraudulent claims using the stolen identities of more than 1 million Americans.

actions.title: Arizona: $1.1 Billion Medically Unnecessary Skin Graft Fraud
actions.description: Prosecutors charged 7 defendants, including 5 medical professionals, in connection with approximately $1.1 billion in fraudulent billing involving medically unnecessary skin grafts billed to Medicare and Medicaid.

actions.title: Pakistan-Based Operator Indicted in $650M Arizona Medicaid Addiction Treatment Fraud
actions.description: Farrukh Jarar Ali, 41, of Pakistan, was indicted for conspiracy to commit health care fraud in connection with a $650 million scheme involving at least 41 sham substance abuse treatment clinics in Arizona. Ali's Pakistan-based company ProMD Solutions credentialed and enrolled these clinics with Arizona Medicaid (AHCCCS) from April 2021 to July 2023, but the clinics did not provide legitimate care. Patients—many recruited from homeless populations and Native American reservations—were offered kickbacks and free room and board. The clinics billed $650 million and received approximately $564 million in payments.

actions.title: DOJ Launches Health Care Fraud Data Fusion Center Using AI and Cloud Analytics
actions.description: Announced as part of the 2025 National Health Care Fraud Takedown, the DOJ launched the Health Care Fraud Data Fusion Center, bringing together experts from the DOJ Health Care Fraud Unit Data Analytics Team, FBI, HHS-OIG, CMS, DEA, and IRS-CI to detect emerging fraud schemes using cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics.
